How it works

Find your rejection reason in under 5 minutes

No coaching fluff. No generic tips. HIRVO runs the real filters recruiters use on your resume, then shows you what failed and how to fix it.

01

Upload your resume

Paste or upload. HIRVO parses it the same way Workday, Greenhouse, and Lever do — then shows you what a recruiter actually receives versus what gets silently dropped.

02

Pick your recruiter type

Startup hiring manager. Enterprise HR screener. University recruiter. Each one screens differently, flags different things, and rejects for different reasons. Pick the one you keep losing to.

03

Read the rejection report

Pass, borderline, or fail — with a specific reason for every verdict. Not “add more keywords.” The recruiter’s actual thought, the exact bullet that failed, and what to rewrite to flip it.

Recruiter Simulation

See exactly how recruiters screen you

Watch a real recruiter screening unfold — from the ATS parse to the final verdict. Every step, every filter, every decision point exposed.

Recruiter simulation

A recruiter spent 7 seconds on your resume. Here's what they saw.

HIRVO models five recruiter types — each with their own filters, gap tolerances, and definitions of "strong." You get the verbatim read: what they noticed, what tripped a flag, and the exact moment they moved on.

  • Verbatim: "what I noticed first" and "why I stopped reading" — no paraphrasing, no softening
  • 5 profiles: startup, enterprise, university, early-career tech, and time-pressured HR screener
  • Skim model built on 6–10 second real-world screening behavior — not career blog assumptions
Recruiter — Startup Hiring Manager
Active model
Select recruiter profile
SH
Startup Hiring ManagerSpeed-focused · Low process tolerance
Active
ET
Early Career TechBrand-conscious · ATS-heavy
HR
HR ScreenerChecklist-driven · Risk-averse
UR
University RecruiterGPA-aware · Pipeline focus
EN
Enterprise Non-TechProcess-heavy · Gap-sensitive
ATS + human scan analysis

Your resume passed ATS. A human still rejected it. Here's why.

Most tools stop at keyword matching. HIRVO shows you what happens next — the human skim — where formatting, weak bullets, and missing signals kill resumes that already passed ATS. The bot approved you. We show you why the human didn't.

  • ATS parse vs. recruiter skim — displayed side by side so the gap is impossible to miss
  • Catches the resumes that pass the bot and get stopped by the human
  • Points to the exact bullet, section, or signal that triggered the reject
ATS vs. Human — Side by Side
Resume evaluation
ATS View
PASS
Human View
STOPPED
Interview simulator

That vague bullet on your resume? A real screener will push back on it. Hard.

HR screens are not conversations — they're structured filters with a checklist and a timer. HIRVO runs yours: follow-up questions on every claim, pushback on unquantified metrics, consistency checks across your timeline. You'll know exactly where you'd fail the screen before it's a real call.

  • Every question is generated from your specific resume — no generic practice prompts
  • Pass / borderline / fail verdict with the explicit reason for every downgrade
  • Timeline inconsistency detection — catches the gaps in your story before a screener does
Screening Interview — HR Screen Mode
Early Career Tech · Round 1 Q 2 of 6
HR
Your resume says you "improved API response time by 40%." Can you walk me through what that actually looked like and how you measured it?
I optimized some database queries and the product manager mentioned it was faster...
YOU
HR
So you don't have a measurement? The 40% figure on your resume — where did that number come from?
Type your response...
Full toolkit

Every filter between you and an interview — modeled.

5 Recruiter Profiles

University, early-career tech, startup, enterprise, and HR screener — each with different red flags, different tolerances, and different reasons to pass on your resume.

ATS Parsing Engine

Parses your resume the way Workday, Greenhouse, and Lever actually do. Shows you what gets extracted cleanly, what gets mangled, and what disappears entirely before a human ever sees your name.

Bullet-Level Scoring

Every bullet gets a signal strength score. Vague impact metrics are flagged. Weak ownership language is called out. You see exactly which lines are dragging your resume down.

Screening Interview Sim

A live, adaptive HR screen built from your actual resume. Pushback on every vague claim. Catches timeline gaps. Tells you where you'd fail the screen — before it's a real call.

International Mode

Visa risk signals, school bias patterns, and sponsorship sensitivity — modeled explicitly. These are the filters that kill international applications before anyone says a word about it.

Progress Tracking

Run the same simulation before and after your edits. See whether your ATS score, readability, and signal strength moved — not just whether the resume looks different.

Methodology

Built on how recruiters actually screen — not how they say they do

HIRVO's recruiter models are built from real screening behavior data — not LinkedIn advice threads or career coaching intuition. Every feedback rule maps to a pattern we've documented in how candidates actually get filtered.

Zero optimism bias
HIRVO doesn't soften the verdict. You get the internal monologue of a recruiter who's seen 200 resumes this week and is already behind schedule.
Role-specific logic
A startup hiring manager doesn't care about GPA. An enterprise screener might reject you for a 4-month gap. Each profile runs different rules on your resume.
Silent filters, made visible
Visa risk flags, school tier bias, career trajectory doubts — HIRVO names the things recruiters act on but never put in a rejection email.
6–10s
Real recruiter skim time — modeled precisely in every simulation
5
Recruiter profiles, each running different rules on your resume
3
Evaluation layers per scan: ATS parse, human skim, deep-read signal
0
Pieces of motivational fluff. Every line of feedback earns its place.
Testimonials

What happens when you finally know why

"

HIRVO flagged that my resume was passing ATS but a recruiter was stopping at the third bullet. I didn't know that was even trackable. Rewrote that bullet, went from zero callbacks to three in two weeks.

JL
Jamie L.
CS Senior · UC Berkeley
"

I'd applied to 60 roles with no responses. HIRVO's international mode showed me I was being flagged for visa sponsorship before anyone even read my name. That was the thing no one ever said out loud.

AK
Arav K.
MIS Grad · Carnegie Mellon
"

The simulator asked me to explain my "team leadership" bullet and I had nothing. I'd written it assuming no one would dig in. Now I know exactly which claims I can't actually defend — and I've fixed them.

RN
Rachel N.
MBA · Stanford GSB
"

HIRVO flagged that my resume was passing ATS but a recruiter was stopping at the third bullet. I didn't know that was even trackable. Rewrote that bullet, went from zero callbacks to three in two weeks.

Jamie L.
CS Senior · UC Berkeley

I'd applied to 60 roles with no responses. HIRVO's international mode showed me I was being flagged for visa sponsorship before anyone even read my name. That was the thing no one ever said out loud.

"

The simulator asked me to explain my "team leadership" bullet and I had nothing. I'd written it assuming no one would dig in. Now I know exactly which claims I can't actually defend — and I've fixed them.

Rachel N.
MBA · Stanford GSB
FAQ

Straight answers. No hedging.

Jobscan tells you whether your resume contains the right keywords. That's useful for exactly one problem: the ATS filter. HIRVO models what happens next — the 6-second human skim, the recruiter's internal read, the follow-up screen — which is where most candidates actually lose. Different problem. More consequential problem.
Yes, and that's the entire point. HIRVO will tell you when a bullet reads like coursework, when "improved performance by 40%" is a number you clearly can't back up, and when a strong experience is buried where no one will see it. No motivational padding. The feedback is exactly what a recruiter would think — written out.
Yes — International Mode is built for exactly this. It models visa risk signals, school tier bias, and sponsorship sensitivity in each recruiter profile. These filters are real, they operate quietly, and most tools ignore them entirely. HIRVO surfaces them with a clear strategy for each one.
Each recruiter profile is modeled on documented screening patterns, not career advice content. We won't claim it perfectly replicates any one recruiter's mind — but when candidates do get honest feedback, it matches what HIRVO already told them.
Three full resume scans, no credit card, no catch. Each scan includes the ATS parse, the human skim simulation, and a complete rejection report with specific reasons and fixes. Any of the five recruiter profiles. Unlimited scans, interview simulation, and progress tracking are on the paid plan.
